name: fog
title: Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) Database
description: Internationally collected, standardized dataset on glacier state and change (length, area, elevation, mass) based on in-situ measurements, remote sensing, and reconstructions.
usageTerms: Open access under the requirement of correct citation
citation: WGMS (2025): Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) Database. World Glacier Monitoring Service (WGMS), Zurich, Switzerland. https://doi.org/10.5904/wgms-fog-2025-02b
publications:
WGMS (2023): Global Glacier Change Bulletin No. 5 (2020-2021). Michael Zemp, Isabelle Gärtner-Roer, Samuel U. Nussbaumer, Ethan Z. Welty, Inés Dussaillant, and Jacqueline Bannwart (eds.), ISC (WDS) / IUGG (IACS) / UNEP / UNESCO / WMO, World Glacier Monitoring Service, Zurich, Switzerland, 134 pp. Based on database version https://doi.org/10.5904/wgms-fog-2023-09.

WGMS (2013): Glacier Mass Balance Bulletin No. 12 (2010-2011). Michael Zemp, Samuel U. Nussbaumer, Kathrin Naegeli, Isabelle Gärtner-Roer, Frank Paul, Martin Hoelzle, and Wilfried Haeberli (eds.), ICSU (WDS) / IUGG (IACS) / UNEP / UNESCO / WMO, World Glacier Monitoring Service, Zurich, Switzerland, 106 pp. Based on database version https://doi.org/10.5904/wgms-fog-2013-11.

WGMS (2012): Fluctuations of Glaciers 2005-2010 (Vol. X): Michael Zemp, Holger Frey, Isabelle Gärtner-Roer, Samuel U. Nussbaumer, Martin Hoelzle, Frank Paul, and Wilfried Haeberli (eds.), ICSU (WDS) / IUGG (IACS) / UNEP / UNESCO / WMO, World Glacier Monitoring Service, Zurich, Switzerland. Based on database version https://doi.org/10.5904/wgms-fog-2012-11.

... and earlier issues (https://wgms.ch/literature_published_by_wgms)
collaborators: WGMS scientific collaboration network of national correspondents and principal investigators as listed in the data (`investigators` and `agencies` columns) and related publications.
disclaimer: The data may contain errors and inaccuracies. Hence, we strongly suggest performing data quality checks and, in case of ambiguities, to contact us as well as the investigators and agencies listed in the data (`investigators` and `agencies` columns) and related publications.

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: short_name
fields.title: Glacier short name
fields.description: The short name only contains characters A-Z, 0-9, - (dash), . (period), : (colon), () (parentheses), / (forward slash), ' (apostrophe), and (space). Non-Latin characters are transliterated, and if the Latin name contains accents, the following rules are typically applied: Å → AA, Æ → AE, Ä → AE, ð → D, Ø → OE, œ → OE, Ö → OE, ß → SS, þ → TH, Ü → UE (neglecting any remaining accents).

fields.name: names
fields.title: Glacier full names
fields.description: Full names (in English and/or local languages) as a pipe-delimited list.

fields.name: id
fields.title: Identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database.

fields.name: latitude
fields.title: Latitude
fields.description: Latitude (EPSG:4326). Positive values indicate the northern hemisphere and negative values indicate the southern hemisphere. The point (`latitude`, `longitude`) should be in the main channel in the upper part of the glacier ablation area.

fields.name: longitude
fields.title: Longitude
fields.description: Longitude (EPSG:4326). Positive values indicate east of the zero meridian and negative values indicate west of the zero meridian. The point (`latitude`, `longitude`) should be in the main channel in the upper part of the glacier ablation area.

fields.name: gtng_region
fields.title: GTN-G region
fields.description: Global Terrestrial Network for Glaciers (GTN-G) 2023 code (https://10.5904/gtng-glacreg-2023-07) of the region in which the glacier is located.

fields.name: glims_id
fields.title: Global Land Ice Measurements from Space (GLIMS) identifier
fields.description: Glacier identifier (`glac_id`) in the Global Land Ice Measurements from Space database (https://www.glims.org). It has the format `GxxxxxxEyyyyyΘ`, where `xxxxxx` is longitude east of the zero meridian in millidegrees, `yyyyy` is north or south latitude in millidegrees, and `Θ` is N or S depending on the hemisphere.

fields.name: rgi50_ids
fields.title: Randolph Glacier Inventory (RGI) 5.0 identifiers
fields.description: Glacier identifiers (`RGIId`), as a pipe-delimited list, from the Randolph Glacier Inventory 5.0 (https://nsidc.org/data/nsidc-0770/versions/5). These have format `RGI50-rr.nnnnn`, where `rr` is the first-order region (zero-padded) and `nnnnn` is an arbitrary numeric code.

fields.name: rgi60_ids
fields.title: Randolph Glacier Inventory (RGI) 6.0 identifiers
fields.description: Glacier identifiers (`RGIId`), as a pipe-delimited list, from the Randolph Glacier Inventory 6.0 (https://nsidc.org/data/nsidc-0770/versions/6). These have format `RGI60-rr.nnnnn`, where `rr` is the first-order region (zero-padded) and `nnnnn` is an arbitrary numeric code.

fields.name: rgi70_ids
fields.title: Randolph Glacier Inventory (RGI) 7.0 identifiers
fields.description: Glacier or glacier complex identifiers (`rgi_id`), as a pipe-delimited list, from the Randolph Glacier Inventory 7.0 (https://nsidc.org/data/nsidc-0770/versions/7). These have format `RGI2000-v7.0-p-rr-nnnnn`, where `p` is the product code (G: glacier or C: complex), `rr` is the first-order region (zero-padded), and `nnnnn` is an arbitrary numeric code.

fields.name: wgi_id
fields.title: World Glacier Inventory (WGI) identifier
fields.description:
Glacier identifier (`wgi_glacier_id`) in the World Glacier Inventory (https://nsidc.org/data/g01130/versions/1). It is constructed from the following elements:

- 2-character political unit
- 1-character continent code
- 4-character drainage code
- 2-character free position code
- 3-character local glacier code

fields.name: parent_glacier_id
fields.title: Parent glacier identifier
fields.description: Identifier (`id`) of the (present or former) parent glacier.

fields.name: references
fields.title: References
fields.example: author YYYY (https://doi.org/doi) | author YYYY (url): title. journal, volume, pages
fields.description: Literature references as a pipe-delimited list.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: Remarks
fields.example: area: Surveyed on 1986-12-31 | [flag] Balance includes unknown calving loss
fields.description: Remarks and flags as a pipe-delimited list.

resources.name: state
resources.title: Glacier state
resources.description: Measurements of glacier state – length, area, elevation, and terminus type.

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: outline_id
fields.title: Glacier outline identifier
fields.description: Glacier outline used to derive the reported measurement(s), as either a GLIMS (https://www.glims.org) analysis id prefixed with 'glims:', RGI (https://www.glims.org/RGI) id, or integer id of a custom outline (available upon request).

fields.name: id
fields.title: State identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier state.

fields.name: date
fields.title: Date
fields.description: Date of the reported measurements.

fields.name: date_unc
fields.title: Date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `date` (e.g. 2020-10-02 ± 1 days = 2020-10-01 to 2020-10-03).

fields.name: highest_elevation
fields.title: Highest glacier elevation
fields.description: Highest elevation on the glacier.

fields.name: lowest_elevation
fields.title: Lowest glacier elevation
fields.description: Lowest elevation on the glacier.

fields.name: mean_elevation
fields.title: Mean glacier elevation
fields.description: Mean glacier elevation.

fields.name: elevation_unc
fields.title: Elevation uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of reported elevations.

fields.name: area
fields.title: Glacier area
fields.description: Glacier area.

fields.name: area_unc
fields.title: Area uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `area`.

fields.name: length
fields.title: Glacier length
fields.description: Glacier length measured along the main flowline.

fields.name: length_unc
fields.title: Length uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `length`.

fields.name: terminus_type
fields.title: Terminus type
fields.description:
Condition at the terminus.
- land: Dry land
- cliff: Very steep dry land (subject to dry calving)
- lake: Lake (freshwater)
- marine: Sea or ocean (saltwater)
- shelf: Ice shelf (which itself terminates in the ocean)

fields.name: platform
fields.title: Survey platform
fields.description:
Survey platform.
- ground: Terrestrial
- air: Airborne
- space: Spaceborne
- other: Other (see `remarks`)

fields.name: method
fields.title: Survey method
fields.description:
Survey method.
- direct: In-situ measurement (e.g. GPS, tape measure)
- map: Maps and mapping
- photo: Photographs and photogrammetry
- laser: Laser scanning
- radar: Radar
- reconstruction: Reconstruction (from historical or dating methods)
- historical: Historical (from texts or visuals)
- text: Text
- print: Print (from an engraving or etching). For photographic prints, use photo.
- painting: Painting
- drawing: Drawing
- radiocarbon: Radiocarbon date
- dendrochronology: Dendrochronology
- surface exposure: Surface exposure dating (e.g. TCN)
- other: Other (see `remarks`)

fields.name: investigators
fields.title: Investigators
fields.description: Pipe-delimited list of persons (linked to `person` table as `person.name`) and their agency affiliations (linked to `agencies` and `agency` table as `agency.id`) responsible for the measurements.

fields.name: agencies
fields.title: Agencies
fields.description: Numbered (by `agency.id`), pipe-delimited list of agencies referred to from `investigators`.
fields.example: 139. World Glacier Monitoring Service | 138. University of Zurich > Department of Geography | 157. Tianshan Glaciological Station

fields.description: Literature references as a pipe-delimited list.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: Remarks
fields.example: area: Surveyed on 1986-12-31 | [flag] Balance includes unknown calving loss
fields.description: Remarks and flags as a pipe-delimited list.

resources.name: change
resources.title: Glacier change
resources.description: Measurements of glacier elevation change – typically from geodetic surveys.

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: begin_outline_id
fields.title: Earlier glacier outline identifier
fields.description: Glacier outline used to derive the glacier state on `begin_date`, as either a GLIMS (https://www.glims.org) analysis id prefixed with 'glims:', RGI (https://www.glims.org/RGI) id, or integer id of a custom outline (available upon request).

fields.name: end_outline_id
fields.title: Later glacier outline identifier
fields.description: Glacier outline used to derive the glacier state on `end_date`, as either a GLIMS (https://www.glims.org) analysis id prefixed with 'glims:', RGI (https://www.glims.org/RGI) id, or integer id of a custom outline (available upon request).

fields.name: id
fields.title: Change identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier change.

fields.name: begin_date
fields.title: Begin date
fields.description: Date of the earlier (reference) measurement.

fields.name: begin_date_unc
fields.title: Begin date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `begin_date` (e.g. 2020-10-02 ± 1 days = 2020-10-01 to 2020-10-03).

fields.name: end_date
fields.title: End date
fields.description: Date of the later measurement.

fields.name: end_date_unc
fields.title: End date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `end_date` (e.g. 2021-10-02 ± 1 days = 2021-10-01 to 2021-10-03).

fields.name: area
fields.title: Area
fields.description: Glacier area (presumably from `begin_date`, `end_date`, or an average) which should be used to estimate `elevation_change` from `volume_change` or vice versa.

fields.name: elevation_change
fields.title: Elevation change
fields.description: Change in surface elevation (averaged over the glacier) from `begin_date` to `end_date`.

fields.name: elevation_change_unc
fields.title: Elevation change uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `elevation_change`.

fields.name: volume_change
fields.title: Volume change
fields.description: Change in glacier volume from `begin_date` to `end_date`.

fields.name: volume_change_unc
fields.title: Volume change uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `volume_change`.

resources.name: front_variation
resources.title: Glacier front variation
resources.description: Measurements of glacier front variation (length change).

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: series_id
fields.title: Series identifier
fields.description: Integer used to distinguish between different front variation series for the same glacier. Value `0` is reserved for the longterm in-situ series (if one exists).

fields.name: begin_date
fields.title: Begin date
fields.description: Date of the earlier (reference) measurement, or null if no reference was used (e.g. glacier advance inferred from moraine condition).

fields.name: begin_date_unc
fields.title: Begin date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `begin_date` (e.g. 2020-10-02 ± 1 days = 2020-10-01 to 2020-10-03).

fields.name: end_date
fields.title: End date
fields.description: Date of the later measurement.

fields.name: end_date_unc
fields.title: End date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `end_date` (e.g. 2021-10-02 ± 1 days = 2021-10-01 to 2021-10-03).

fields.name: length_change
fields.title: Length change
fields.description: Change in glacier length from `begin_date` to `end_date` (+ advance, - retreat).

fields.name: length_change_unc
fields.title: Length change uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `length_change`.

fields.name: length_change_direction
fields.title: Length change direction
fields.description:
Length change direction (in the absence of a quantitative `length_change`) from `begin_date` to `end_date`.
- advance: Glacier advanced (+)
- retreat: Glacier retreated (-)
- stationary: Glacier was stationary (0)

- other: Other (see `remarks`)

resources.name: mass_balance
resources.title: Glacier mass balance
resources.description: Glacier-wide mass balance and survey details.

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: outline_id
fields.title: Glacier outline identifier
fields.description: Glacier outline used to derive the reported glacier-wide mass balance(s), as either a GLIMS (https://www.glims.org) analysis id prefixed with 'glims:', RGI (https://www.glims.org/RGI) id, or integer id of a custom outline (available upon request).

fields.name: year
fields.title: Hydrological year
fields.description: Calendar year associated with the last accumulation (winter) - ablation (summer) cycle. This is almost always the calendar year at the end of the measurement period unless the cycle extends only briefly into the following year (e.g. 2020-01-05 to 2021-01-17 ends in 2021 but is the 2020 hydrological year).

fields.name: time_system
fields.title: Time measurement system
fields.description:
Time measurement system of the dates (`begin_date`, `midseason_date` and `end_date`) and balances (`winter_balance`, `summer_balance`, `annual_balance`). See Cogley 2011 (https://unesdoc.unesco.org/ark:/48223/pf0000192525) for background.
- floating: Floating-date. Dates are the dates of measurement, and balances are as measured. [Introduced in 2011, so earlier balances based on this system are theoretically reported as 'other'. Redefined in 2024 to include stratigraphic measurements.]
- fixed: Fixed-date. Dates are those to which the measured balances were corrected (typically the hydrological year), and balances are as corrected. Correction methods should be given in the `remarks`. [Redefined to include stratigraphic measurements in 2024]
- stratigraphic: Stratigraphic. Involves measurements between successive minimum or maximum surfaces (at least in the accumulation area), often in combination with fixed or floating-date measurements in the ablation area. [Discontinued in 2024]
- combined: Some combination of the above (see `remarks`). [Discontinued in 2024]
- other: Other (see `remarks`).

fields.name: begin_date
fields.title: Begin date
fields.description: Begin (reference) date of the reported `winter_balance` and `annual_balance`.

fields.name: begin_date_unc
fields.title: Begin date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `begin_date` (e.g. 2020-10-02 ± 1 days = 2020-10-01 to 2020-10-03).

fields.name: midseason_date
fields.title: Midseason date
fields.description: End date of the reported `winter_balance` and begin date of the reported `summer_balance`.

fields.name: midseason_date_unc
fields.title: Midseason date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `midseason_date` (e.g. 2021-05-04 ± 2 days = 2021-05-02 to 2021-05-06).

fields.name: end_date
fields.title: End date
fields.description: End date of the reported `summer_balance` and `annual_balance`.

fields.name: end_date_unc
fields.title: End date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `end_date` (e.g. 2021-10-02 ± 1 days = 2021-10-01 to 2021-10-03).

fields.name: winter_balance
fields.title: Winter mass balance
fields.description: Mass balance over the winter (accumulation) season – from `begin_date` to `midseason_date`.

fields.name: winter_balance_unc
fields.title: Winter mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `winter_balance`.

fields.name: summer_balance
fields.title: Summer mass balance
fields.description: Mass balance over the summer (ablation) season – from `midseason_date` to `end_date`.

fields.name: summer_balance_unc
fields.title: Summer mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `summer_balance`.

fields.name: annual_balance
fields.title: Annual mass balance
fields.description: Mass balance over the hydrological year – from `begin_date` to `end_date`.

fields.name: annual_balance_unc
fields.title: Annual mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `annual_balance`.

fields.name: ela_position
fields.title: Equilibrium line altitude (ELA) position
fields.description:
Position of the equilibrium line altitude (ELA) relative to the glacier.
- on: On the glacier
- above: Above the glacier (`ela` may be a lower bound)
- below: Below the glacier (`ela` may be an upper bound)

fields.name: ela
fields.title: Equilibrium line altitude (ELA)
fields.description: Mean elevation, averaged over the glacier, of the end-of-mass-balance-year equilibrium line. This can be outside the elevation range of the glacier if the `ela_position` was `below` or `above`.

fields.name: ela_unc
fields.title: Equilibrium line altitude (ELA) uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `ela`.

fields.name: aar
fields.title: Accumulation area ratio (AAR)
fields.description: Accumulation area divided by the total glacier area.

fields.name: area
fields.title: Area
fields.description: Area considered in the calculation of the glacier-wide mass balance.

fields.name: investigators
fields.title: Investigators
fields.description: Pipe-delimited list of persons (linked to `person` table as `person.name`) and their agency affiliations (linked to `agencies` and `agency` table as `agency.id`) responsible for the measurements.

fields.name: agencies
fields.title: Agencies
fields.description: Numbered (by `agency.id`), pipe-delimited list of agencies referred to from `investigators`.
fields.example: 139. World Glacier Monitoring Service | 138. University of Zurich > Department of Geography | 157. Tianshan Glaciological Station

fields.name: references
fields.title: References
fields.example: author YYYY (https://doi.org/doi) | author YYYY (url): title. journal, volume, pages
fields.description: Literature references as a pipe-delimited list.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: Remarks
fields.example: area: Surveyed on 1986-12-31 | [flag] Balance includes unknown calving loss
fields.description: Remarks and flags as a pipe-delimited list.

resources.name: mass_balance_band
resources.title: Glacier mass balance by elevation band
resources.description: Glacier mass balance by elevation band.

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: year
fields.title: Hydrological year
fields.description: Calendar year associated with the last accumulation (winter) - ablation (summer) cycle. This is almost always the calendar year at the end of the measurement period unless the cycle extends only briefly into the following year (e.g. 2020-01-05 to 2021-01-17 ends in 2021 but is the 2020 hydrological year).

fields.name: lower_elevation
fields.title: Lower elevation bound
fields.description: Lower boundary of the surface elevation band.

fields.name: upper_elevation
fields.title: Upper elevation bound
fields.description: Upper boundary of the surface elevation band.

fields.name: area
fields.title: Area
fields.description: Area of the elevation band.

fields.name: winter_balance
fields.title: Winter mass balance
fields.description: Mass balance over the winter (accumulation) season – from `mass_balance.begin_date` to `mass_balance.midseason_date`.

fields.name: winter_balance_unc
fields.title: Winter mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `winter_balance`.

fields.name: summer_balance
fields.title: Summer mass balance
fields.description: Mass balance over the summer (ablation) season – from `mass_balance.midseason_date` to `mass_balance.end_date`.

fields.name: summer_balance_unc
fields.title: Summer mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `summer_balance`.

fields.name: annual_balance
fields.title: Annual mass balance
fields.description: Mass balance over the hydrological year – from `mass_balance.begin_date` to `mass_balance.end_date`.

fields.name: annual_balance_unc
fields.title: Annual mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `annual_balance`.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: Remarks
fields.example: area: Surveyed on 1986-12-31 | [flag] Balance includes unknown calving loss
fields.description: Remarks and flags as a pipe-delimited list.

resources.name: mass_balance_point
resources.title: Glacier mass balance by point
resources.description: Glacier mass balance measured at specific points (e.g. stakes or pits).

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: year
fields.title: Hydrological year
fields.description: Calendar year associated with the last accumulation (winter) - ablation (summer) cycle. This is almost always the calendar year at the end of the measurement period unless the cycle extends only briefly into the following year (e.g. 2020-01-05 to 2021-01-17 ends in 2021 but is the 2020 hydrological year).

fields.name: id
fields.title: Point identifier
fields.description: Unique identifier for the point.

fields.name: original_id
fields.title: Original identifier
fields.description: Identifier for the point used by the original investigators.

fields.name: begin_date
fields.title: Begin date
fields.description: Begin (reference) date of the reported `balance`.

fields.name: begin_date_unc
fields.title: Begin date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated range of `begin_date` (e.g. 2020-10-02 ± 1 days = 2020-10-01 to 2020-10-03).

fields.name: end_date
fields.title: End date
fields.description: End date of the reported `balance`.

fields.name: end_date_unc
fields.title: End date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated range of `end_date` (e.g. 2021-10-02 ± 1 days = 2021-10-01 to 2021-10-03).

fields.name: latitude
fields.title: Latitude
fields.description: Point latitude (EPSG:4326). Positive values indicate the northern hemisphere and negative values indicate the southern hemisphere.

fields.name: longitude
fields.title: Longitude
fields.description: Point longitude (EPSG:4326). Positive values indicate east of the zero meridian and negative values indicate west of the zero meridian.

fields.name: elevation
fields.title: Elevation
fields.description: Glacier surface elevation at point.

fields.name: balance
fields.title: Mass balance
fields.description: Point mass balance from `begin_date` to `end_date` (+ accumulation, - ablation).

fields.name: balance_unc
fields.title: Mass balance uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `balance`.

fields.name: density
fields.title: Density
fields.description: Measured or estimated density used to convert height change to mass balance. If multiple density values were used (e.g. for snow and ice), they should be described in `remarks`.

fields.name: density_unc
fields.title: Density uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated random error of `density`.

fields.name: balance_code
fields.title: Balance code
fields.description:
Whether and how the point balance was used in the calculation of glacier-wide (`mass_balance`) and elevation band (`mass_balance_band`) balance.
- annual: Used for annual balance.
- summer: Used for summer balance.
- winter: Used for winter balance.
- index: Not used (index measurement).

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: Remarks
fields.example: area: Surveyed on 1986-12-31 | [flag] Balance includes unknown calving loss
fields.description: Remarks and flags as a pipe-delimited list.

resources.name: event
resources.title: Event
resources.description: Noteworthy events concerning glacier hazards and dramatic glacier changes.

fields.name: country
fields.title: Country
fields.description: ISO 3166 alpha-2 code (https://www.iso.org/obp/ui) of the country in which the glacier is (primarily) located.

fields.name: glacier_name
fields.title: Glacier name
fields.description: Short name of the glacier (see `glacier.short_name`).

fields.name: glacier_id
fields.title: Glacier identifier
fields.description: Integer identifying the glacier in the Fluctuations of Glaciers (FoG) database (see `glacier.id`).

fields.name: id
fields.title: Event identifier
fields.description: Integer indentifying the event.

fields.name: date
fields.title: Date
fields.description: Date of the event. Events that span multiple days or represent a series of events should be further described with dates in `description`.

fields.name: date_unc
fields.title: Date uncertainty
fields.description: Estimated (or exact) range of `date` (e.g. 2020-10-02 ± 1 days = 2020-10-01 to 2020-10-03).

fields.name: latitude
fields.title: Latitude
fields.description: Latitude (EPSG:4326). Positive values indicate the northern hemisphere and negative values indicate the southern hemisphere. The point (`latitude`, `longitude`) should be as close as possible to the event source.

fields.name: longitude
fields.title: Longitude
fields.description: Longitude (EPSG:4326). Positive values indicate east of the zero meridian and negative values indicate west of the zero meridian. The point (`latitude`, `longitude`) should be as close as possible to the event source.

fields.name: description
fields.title: Description
fields.example: On 8 September 2000, a rock fall of about 0.1 million m3 started from 2000–2200 m on the west face of Mättenberg, above Findelen Glacier. The rockfall reached and destroyed the trail leading to the Schreckhornhütte.
fields.description:
Summary description of the event sequence - including for example the type and scale of the damage, measures taken to mitigate glacier hazards, and studies carried out in connection with the event. Quantitative information should be included whenever possible.

  - Surge: Date and location of onset, duration, flow velocity, discharge anomalies and periodicity
  - Calving: Rate of retreat, iceberg discharge, flow velocity and water depth at calving front
  - Flood: Volume, mechanism, peak discharge, sediment load, reach and propagation velocity of flood wave or flow front
  - Ice avalanche: Volume, runout distance, overall slope (ratio of vertical drop height to horizontal runout distance) of path
  - Tectonics: Volumes, runout distances and overall slopes (ratio of vertical drop height to horizontal runout distance) of rockfall on glacier surface, amount of geothermal melting in craters, etc.

fields.name: surge
fields.title: Surge
fields.description: Whether a surge was involved.

fields.name: calving
fields.title: Calving
fields.description: Whether calving was involved.

fields.name: flood
fields.title: Flood
fields.description: Whether a flood (e.g. glacial-lake outburst flood) was involved.

fields.name: avalanche
fields.title: Avalanche
fields.description: Whether an ice avalanche was involved.

fields.name: rockfall
fields.title: Rockfall
fields.description: Whether rockfall was involved.

fields.name: debris_flow
fields.title: Debris flow
fields.description: Whether a debris flow was involved.

fields.name: earthquake
fields.title: Earthquake
fields.description: Whether an earthquake was involved.

fields.name: volcanic_eruption
fields.title: Volcanic eruption
fields.description: Whether a volcanic eruption was involved.

fields.name: other
fields.title: Other
fields.description: Whether any other event types were involved.
